    "Drain line contains a back-graded section confirmed by puddle test, creating a low point that will retain wastewater and solids.",
    "Standing water in the drain line violates gravity drainage slope requirements and will cause blockages, odors, and impaired drainage performance."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and re-pitch the affected drain section to provide continuous positive slope toward the point of disposal.",
    "Re-test with puddle test or level verification to confirm no low spots remain before closure.",
    "Coordinate re-inspection with the third-party inspector."
  ],
  "rationale": "IPC 704 requires drainage piping to be sloped to drain. A back-graded section that holds water (positive puddle test) is a clear non-compliance: it prevents self-scouring flow, creates a blockage and odor hazard, and fails the fundamental gravity drainage slope requirement. Because the work is rough-complete, it must be corrected before the system is closed out or accepted.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 704"
  ]
}
